perturbation.original_text: RECONCILIATION/PAYMENT TERMS: The Parties agree to reconcile revenue numbers and the revenue share calculations no later than 10 days following the end of each calendar month during which the Services were rendered. Subject to the doctrine of sequential liability, payment shall be made to the appropriate Party net 30 days from the end of the calendar month.
perturbation.changed_text: RECONCILIATION/PAYMENT TERMS: The Parties agree to reconcile revenue numbers and the revenue share calculations no later than 10 days following the end of each calendar month during which the Services were rendered. Subject to the doctrine of sequential liability, payment shall be made to the appropriate Party net 30 days from the date of the invoice, which will be submitted within 5 days from the end of each calendar month.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment deadline calculation. The original text stipulates 'net 30 days from the end of the calendar month.' The modification specifies 'net 30 days from the date of the invoice, which will be submitted within 5 days from the end of each calendar month.' This creates an inconsistency. Depending on when the invoice is sent within those 5 days, payment is effectively required sooner than the original 30 days from the end of the month, leading to confusion and disputes regarding the agreed-upon payment timeframe.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECONCILIATION/PAYMENT TERMS: The Parties agree to reconcile revenue numbers and the revenue share calculations no later than 10 days following the end of each calendar month during which the Services were rendered. Subject to the doctrine of sequential liability, payment shall be made to the appropriate Party net 30 days from the date of the invoice, which will be submitted within 5 days from the end of each calendar month.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction within the 'RECONCILIATION/PAYMENT TERMS' section. The original text specifies payment 'net 30 days from the end of the calendar month,' while the modified text stipulates 'net 30 days from the date of the invoice, which will be submitted within 5 days from the end of each calendar month.' The contradicted text is the changed text itself. This alteration creates a conflict as the payment deadline is no longer a fixed date (30 days after month end) but dependent on when the invoice is issued within the 5-day window, potentially shortening the payment period, leading to uncertainty and disputes regarding the payment timeline.

perturbation.original_text: CONFLICTING TERMS: The Schedule 1 shall supersede the flat fee and Initial Revenue Share set forth in Exhibit A. The payment term provision above shall supersede the payment term provision set forth in Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: CONFLICTING TERMS: The Schedule 1 shall supersede the flat fee and Initial Revenue Share set forth in Exhibit A. The payment term provision above shall not supersede the payment term provision set forth in Exhibit A.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, the payment terms in Exhibit B superseded those in Exhibit A. Now, the statement says they do NOT supersede Exhibit A's payment terms. This creates direct conflict and ambiguity. The affected location highlights the conflict: it is now unclear which payment terms actually apply, and the contract is self-contradictory. This will lead to significant disagreement between both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFLICTING TERMS: The Schedule 1 shall supersede the flat fee and Initial Revenue Share set forth in Exhibit A. The payment term provision above shall not supersede the payment term provision set forth in Exhibit A.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. Originally, the payment term provision in Schedule 1 superseded the payment term provision in Exhibit A. The modified text states that the payment term provision in Schedule 1 does NOT supersede the payment term provision in Exhibit A. This creates a clear and direct conflict, because it is now unclear which payment terms apply, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
